title: humboltia recurva
intro: Humboltia recurva, also known as the climbing Humboldtia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka and India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Humboltia recurva include its ability to treat fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and to treat digestive problems. Humboltia recurva is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional medicine as a paste or powder.
